name: Reserva Natural de Llaberia – circular desde Montroig del Camp por el castillo de Escornalbou
region_intro: Mountain bike trails around Mont-Roig Del Camp traverse a diverse landscape, characterized by Mediterranean forests, rugged inland terrain, and a 12-kilometer coastline. The region offers a mix of challenging climbs and scenic coastal paths, providing varied riding experiences. A dedicated Mountain Bike Center serves as an information hub, detailing numerous marked routes and offering services for cyclists. This blend of natural beauty and infrastructure makes Mont-Roig Del Camp a notable destination for mountain biking. Best mountain bike trails around Mont-Roig Del Camp The most popular mountain bike trail is Reserva Natural de Llaberia – circular desde Montroig del Camp por el castillo de Escornalbou, a 25.3 miles (40.7 km) difficult trail that takes 4 hours 16 minutes to complete, offering views of the Llaberia Natural Reserve and Escornalbou Castle. Another top favourite among local mountain bikers is Vilanova d'Escornalbou – Cal Ambo loop from Bus Stop ( L'Hospitalet de l'infant - Cambrils), a moderate 25.5 miles (41.1 km) path that explores the inland areas with varied terrain. Local mountain bikers also love the Cambrils Seafront Promenade – Cambrils cycle path loop from Reception, a 19.1 miles (30.7 km) trail leading through coastal areas and along the seafront promenade, often completed in about 1 hour 46 minutes. Mountain biking around Mont-Roig Del Camp is defined by Mediterranean forests, coastal paths, and rugged inland terrain. The network offers options for different ability levels, from easy coastal rides to challenging climbs. The routes in Mont-Roig Del Camp are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.5 stars from over 200 reviews. More than 1500 mountain bikers have used komoot to explore Mont-Roig Del Camp's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many mountain bike trails are available in Mont-Roig Del Camp?
A: Mont-Roig Del Camp offers a wide selection of mountain bike trails, with over 100 routes recorded on komoot. Additionally, the dedicated Mont-roig del Camp Mountain Bike Center provides details on 10 marked routes, catering to various skill levels.

Q: Are there mountain bike trails suitable for beginners or families?
A: Yes, Mont-Roig Del Camp has plenty of options for less experienced riders and families. There are 36 easy mountain bike routes available. A good example is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/40381361" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cambrils Seafront Promenade – Cambrils cycle path loop from Reception&lt;/a&gt;, which is 19.1 miles (30.7 km) long and leads through coastal areas.

Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on the mountain bike trails around Mont-Roig Del Camp?
A: The terrain in Mont-Roig Del Camp is quite varied. You'll find a mix of typical Mediterranean forests, rugged inland areas with more challenging climbs, and scenic coastal paths along the 12-kilometer coastline. This diversity provides different riding experiences, from gentle seaside rides to more demanding routes in the hills.

Q: Are there any circular mountain bike routes in the area?
A: Yes, many of the mountain bike routes in Mont-Roig Del Camp are circular, allowing you to start and end at the same point. For example, the challenging &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e945397698/reserva-natural-de-llaberia-circular-desde-montroig-del-camp-por-el-castillo-de-escornalbou" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Reserva Natural de Llaberia – circular desde Montroig del Camp por el castillo de Escornalbou&lt;/a&gt; is a 25.3-mile (40.7 km) loop offering views of the Llaberia Natural Reserve and Escornalbou Castle.

Q: What are some notable landmarks or viewpoints I can see along the trails?
A: The trails offer access to several interesting landmarks and viewpoints. You can visit the 13th-century &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3064827" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Hermitage of Mare de Déu de la Roca&lt;/a&gt;, which provides impressive views. Other points of interest include &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5137425" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Sant Miquel d'Escornalbou Castle and Monastery&lt;/a&gt;, and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2558939" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Rifà Bridge&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: What do other mountain bikers enjoy most about Mont-Roig Del Camp?
A: The mountain bike trails in Mont-Roig Del Camp are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.5 stars from over 260 reviews. Riders often praise the diverse landscape, which combines Mediterranean forests, rugged inland terrain, and beautiful coastal paths, offering varied experiences for all skill levels.

Q: Is Mont-Roig Del Camp suitable for advanced mountain bikers?
A: Absolutely. For advanced mountain bikers, there are 18 difficult routes available. These trails often feature significant elevation gains and technical sections. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e945397698/reserva-natural-de-llaberia-circular-desde-montroig-del-camp-por-el-castillo-de-escornalbou" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Reserva Natural de Llaberia – circular desde Montroig del Camp por el castillo de Escornalbou&lt;/a&gt;, for instance, involves over 1200 meters of ascent.

Q: Are there any services for mountain bikers in Mont-Roig Del Camp?
A: Yes, Mont-Roig Del Camp is well-equipped for mountain bikers. The dedicated Mont-roig del Camp Mountain Bike Center serves as an information hub and offers services such as bike rental for adults and children, bike washing facilities, and showers.

Q: What is the best time of year to go mountain biking in Mont-Roig Del Camp?
A: Mont-Roig Del Camp generally enjoys clear skies and a Mediterranean climate, making it suitable for mountain biking for much of the year. Spring and autumn typically offer the most pleasant temperatures for riding, avoiding the peak heat of summer while still providing good weather conditions.

Q: Are there any moderate mountain bike trails for intermediate riders?
A: Yes, intermediate riders will find a great selection of routes, with 48 moderate trails available. A popular choice is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/17521497" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Vilanova d'Escornalbou – Cal Ambo loop from Bus Stop ( L'Hospitalet de l'infant - Cambrils)&lt;/a&gt;, a 25.5-mile (41.1 km) path that explores inland areas with varied terrain.

Q: Can I find mountain bike routes that include coastal views?
A: Definitely. Mont-Roig Del Camp boasts a 12-kilometer coastline, and several mountain bike routes incorporate these scenic coastal areas. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/40381361" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cambrils Seafront Promenade – Cambrils cycle path loop from Reception&lt;/a&gt; is a great option for enjoying sea views.

Q: What other outdoor activities are available in Mont-Roig Del Camp besides mountain biking?
A: Beyond mountain biking, Mont-Roig Del Camp offers a wide range of outdoor pursuits. You can enjoy hiking, road cycling, gravel biking, and various water sports along the coast, such as windsurfing, kayaking, and paddle surfing. Horse riding and golf are also popular options.
tips: Beautiful views of Mont-roig del Camp, a town located between the Sierra de Colldejou and the Mediterranean Sea. In the center of the municipality stands the parish church of Sant Miquel.

A beautiful hermitage, built in the 16th century, which stands out for being integrated into the red mountain of La Roca, from where you can see wonderful views of Baix Camp and the Costa Daurada. The space also has a hospitality service that offers restaurant service and room rental.

The Castillo Monasterio de Escornalbou de Riudecanyes is a stately mansion from the beginning of the 20th century formed by the remains of two medieval buildings: the castle, built on the remains of a Roman fortress, and the monastery of Sant Miquel.

waypoints.name: Our Lady of the Rock Hermitage
Sant Miquel d'Escornalbou Castle and Monastery
